chunks.page_summary: This page details the partnership between Rheonics and the PROFIBUS and PROFINET International (PI) association, highlighting Rheonics' role as a vendor of PROFINET-compatible systems and the support provided for their integration.

PROFIBUS and PROFINET International (PI)
association and Rheonics

Modified on: Thu, 21 Dec, 2023 at 12:59 AM

The PROFIBUS and PROFINET International (PI) association is one of the largest automation organizations in the
world and is responsible for PROFIBUS and PROFINET, two important automation technologies.
Rheonics is a member of the PI association as a vendor of hardware, software, and PROFINET-compatible systems.
Check our membership at this link: Members (https://www.profibus.com/pi-organization/members?tx_solr%5Bq%5D=rheonics#tx_solr-results)

Rheonics provides detailed user manuals and technical support for integration. Our PROFINET-compatible sensors
can be easily integrated into PROFINET networks thanks to our support portal's guidelines and steps. In addition,
our experienced engineering team can assist with installation and configuration to ensure successful integration into
your PROFINET network on time. Visit our Support portal for more information on PROFINET Protocol: PROFINE T (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000292663)

chunks.page_summary: This page provides an overview of the PROFINEТ protocol, detailing its benefits for industrial processes, its technical specifications as an open industrial Ethernet system, and how it functions for both time-critical and non-time-critical applications, particularly in the context of Rheonics' viscometers and density sensors.
chunks.extracted_text:
2. What is the PROFINEТ protocol?
3. How does the PROFINEТ protocol work?
4. Rheonics process viscometer and density meters have native Profinet support
5. Rheonics Expertise
6. What is available over PROFINEТ?
7. Resources
8. References

1. How does this partnership benefit your process?
PROFINEТ offers these advantages for the viscosity and density monitoring:
*   Flexibility: PROFINEТ IO accommodates the current plant setup by incorporating versatile line, ring, and star structures, thereby negating the necessity for costly bespoke alternatives. This solution relies entirely on standard Ethernet, facilitating seamless integration of wired and wireless transmission physics. Consequently, WLAN and Bluetooth communication can be seamlessly integrated into the solution, even for real-time data.
*   Increased availability: PROFINEТ IO incorporates redundancy measures, encompassing IT-based network redundancy and seamless switchover operations for rapid control processes, as well as control system redundancy for uninterrupted process-related applications. Furthermore, a versatile diagnostics concept provides crucial information about network topology and the status of all devices, ensuring cross-vendor, location-specific diagnostics. The inclusion of mechanisms for preventive diagnostics enhances overall system availability.
*   Uniform integration on fieldbus systems: To incorporate current device structures with fieldbuses, the modular device model of PROFINEТ IO has undergone standardization through fieldbus-specific proxy specifications. This guarantees uniform addressing, parameter assignment, and diagnostics for the underlying fieldbus network.
*   Enabler for Industrie 4.0: The future-proof structure of PROFINEТ enables the implementation of industrial 4.0 applications today. Emerging technologies like TSN can be seamlessly integrated and employed.

2. What is the PROFINEТ protocol?
PROFINEТ is an open industrial Ethernet system based on international standards and part of IEC 61158 / IEC 61784-2, which defines what the signal looks like on the wire and what the medium accessing the wire has to do, it also uses industrial Ethernet as the physical layer (defined by IEEE802.3). It is a real-time communication protocol, meaning that devices can exchange data in real time with minimal delays. It is also a deterministic protocol, meaning that data is exchanged with predictable latency.

Using PROFINEТ, components can communicate cyclically or acyclically, and exchange diagnostics, safety functions, alarms, and additional information. As a communication medium, PROFINEТ uses Ethernet to link all of those components. PROFINEТ components can be connected within a network alongside other Ethernet protocols such as OPC UA, SNMP, MQTT, or HTTP, which can be used to complement PROFINEТ.

3. How does the PROFINEТ protocol work?
PROFINEТ can utilize TCP/IP or UDP/IP communication for tasks that are not time-critical, such as configuration and parameterization. However, due to the introduced latency and jitter associated with IP-based communication, this approach is not suitable for time-critical operations.

To address time-critical applications, PROFINEТ implements a real-time channel, ensuring the rapid and deterministic delivery of data.
Here's how PROFINEТ real-time functions: Standard Ethernet frames include a field known as EtherType, indicating the protocol type.
key_facts: PROFINEТ IO offers flexibility for viscosity and density monitoring by accommodating existing plant setups and integrating various transmission physics, including WLAN and Bluetooth for real-time data.
PROFINEТ IO enhances system availability through IT-based network redundancy, seamless switchover for rapid control processes, and comprehensive diagnostics for network topology and device status.

PROFINEТ IO ensures uniform integration on fieldbus systems via standardized modular device models and fieldbus-specific proxy specifications, guaranteeing uniform addressing, parameter assignment, and network diagnostics.
PROFINEТ's future-proof structure facilitates the implementation of Industrial 4.0 applications and supports the seamless integration of emerging technologies like TSN.
PROFINEТ is an open industrial Ethernet system based on international standards IEC 61158 and IEC 61784-2.
PROFINEТ uses industrial Ethernet as its physical layer (IEEE802.3) and operates as a real-time and deterministic communication protocol, ensuring minimal delays and predictable latency.
PROFINEТ components can communicate cyclically or acyclically, exchanging diagnostics, safety functions, alarms, and additional information.
PROFINEТ can coexist with other Ethernet protocols like OPC UA, SNMP, MQTT, or HTTP within the same network.
For non-time-critical tasks like configuration and parameterization, PROFINEТ can use TCP/IP or UDP/IP communication.
To ensure rapid and deterministic data delivery for time-critical applications, PROFINEТ implements a dedicated real-time channel.
PROFINEТ real-time functions are identified within standard Ethernet frames by a specific EtherType field.

chunks.page_summary: This page details the architecture of PROFINET real-time communication and explains how Rheonics inline viscometers and density sensors leverage native PROFINET support for enhanced industrial automation applications.
chunks.extracted_text:
PROFINET real-time communication is configured with an EtherType of 0x8892. Upon reaching the destination node, the frame is promptly directed to the PROFINET application. The data travels directly from Ethernet Layer 2 to PROFINET Layer 7, bypassing the TCP/IP layers and circumventing potential variability in processing time. Consequently, communication speed and determinism experience a significant enhancement.

5-7: Session/Presentation/Application
Profinet services (I/O, alarms, records, etc.)

4 Transport layer
UDP
TCP

3: Network layer
IP

2: Data Link Layer
RT
IRT
TSN

1: Physical layer
100 Mbit/s, 1 Gbit Mbit/s

Figure 2. PROFINET transportation layer.

4. Rheonics process viscometer and density meters have native Profinet support
In diverse automation applications, PROFINET plays a pivotal role by ensuring smooth communication between an array of field devices and the central control system, with a special emphasis on its integration with PLCs.

Rheonics process automation sensors with PROFINET communication facilitates remote access to comprehensive device diagnostics and configurations, resulting in swift decision-making, reduced downtime, and an overall enhancement of system efficiency.

Having profinet directly on the sensor removes the need for using converters and infield programming. Now customers can drop the GDSML file directly on the PLC and start communicating over profinet with the Rheonics sensor on their industrial network.

5. Rheonics Expertise
A few typical applications where our customers are using PROFINET protocol are:

*   Transmission of density (weight) of drilling mud over PROFINET.
*   The viscosity of batter mix that is applied to various appetizer food products.
*   Plywood glue manufacturing and application - measure and transmit viscosity and temperature of glue.
*   Measure and transmit viscosity in the process for water-based ink.
key_facts: PROFINET real-time communication is configured with an EtherType of 0x8892.
PROFINET data travels directly from Ethernet Layer 2 to PROFINET Layer 7, bypassing TCP/IP layers, which enhances communication speed and determinism.
Rheonics process viscometers and density meters have native PROFINET support.
PROFINET facilitates smooth communication between field devices and central control systems, with emphasis on PLC integration.
Rheonics sensors with PROFINET communication allow remote access to device diagnostics and configurations, leading to faster decision-making, reduced downtime, and improved system efficiency.
Native PROFINET on Rheonics sensors eliminates the need for converters and infield programming.
Customers can drop the GDSML file directly onto the PLC to communicate with Rheonics sensors over PROFINET.

Rheonics' PROFINET protocol is used for applications such as transmitting density of drilling mud, measuring viscosity of batter mix, measuring and transmitting viscosity and temperature of plywood glue, and measuring and transmitting viscosity for water-based ink.

chunks.page_summary: This page details the applications and measurements available via PROFINET communication for Rheonics inline viscometers and density meters, providing extensive resources and references for integration.
chunks.extracted_text:
• Measure and transmit the density, viscosity, and temperature of ceramic slurry from the slurry drum to the PLC and SCADA system in shell rooms.
• Monitoring and transmission of density, viscosity, and temperature to SCADA system over PROFINET in Processing plants for food production as well as in the beverage industry and dairies
• Automation and control for water/wastewater treatment uses PLC with PROFINET communication to get sewage water density and viscosity for nitrification, polyelectrolyte, and calamity detection.
• and many more.

6. What is available over PROFINET?
All measurements from the Rheonics inline process density meter and viscosity meter are accessible over profinet. This includes density, viscosity, kinematic viscosity, and temperature.

We also offer advanced calculation models for compensated viscosity, compensated density, and concentration which can be transmitted over PROFINET along with the line measurements. Learn how you can get °API, °Baume, compensated viscosity, and density using the onboard MATHS engine > Measurements (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/81000186361)

7. Resources
For more information about how Rheonics has implemented PROFINET in all their devices visit:
1. PROFINET Fundamentals (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000410678-profinet-fundamentals)
2. Getting started with PROFINET and Rheonics devices (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000410679-getting-started-with-profinet-on-rheonics-devices)
3. Taking the PROFINET road with Rheonics SME (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411227-taking-the-profinet-road-with-rheonics-sme)
4. Connecting the Profinet output (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000401596-connecting-the-profinet-output)
5. Profinet connection to inline viscometer and density meter (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000401423-profinet-gsd-file-for-rheonics-inline-viscometer-and-density-meter)
6. Software for Profinet integration with Rheonics Sensors (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411197-software-for-profinet-integration-with-rheonics-sensors)

PROFINET enables monitoring and transmission of density, viscosity, and temperature to SCADA systems in processing plants for food production, beverage industry, and dairies.

PROFINET communication with PLC is used for automation and control in water/wastewater treatment to obtain sewage water density and viscosity for nitrification, polyelectrolyte, and calamity detection.
All measurements from Rheonics inline process density meters and viscosity meters, including density, viscosity, kinematic viscosity, and temperature, are accessible over PROFINET.
Rheonics offers advanced calculation models for compensated viscosity, compensated density, and concentration, which can be transmitted over PROFINET.
The onboard MATHS engine allows users to obtain °API, °Baume, compensated viscosity, and density measurements.
